_note: Editorial content for each committee, keyed by committee id (matches committees.json). 'tldr' is the short hover summary shown on cards; 'purpose' is a 1-2 sentence framing; 'responsibilities' are the official duties (sourced from the UCF Faculty Senate committee pages, https://facultysenate.ucf.edu/committees/); 'cluster' groups committees on the /how-it-works/ governance map; 'reportsTo' is the body each committee forwards its recommendations to. Editing this file does NOT affect signup matching — only committees.json ids do that.

academic-calendar.tldr: Sets the university's academic calendar and weighs how calendar changes affect students and faculty.
academic-calendar.purpose: Recommends the academic calendar for the university and considers the downstream impact of any calendar change on the people it affects.
responsibilities: Recommend the academic calendar and ensure compliance with the rules and regulations of the State of Florida and the university Board of Trustees.
Consider the impact of changes in the academic calendar on students and faculty.
academic-calendar.reportsTo: Faculty Senate (via the Steering Committee) and the Board of Trustees

faculty-center-advisory.tldr: Guides faculty teaching and learning development through the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning.
faculty-center-advisory.purpose: Advises the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning on professional development that strengthens teaching and learning for all faculty.
responsibilities: Support and develop faculty professional development in teaching, learning, and the scholarship of teaching for all faculty, including full-time, adjunct, and graduate teaching assistants.
Oversee and administer professional teaching development activities.
Coordinate the nomination, review, and selection of faculty fellowships and other awards for creative teaching and the innovative use of technology in teaching and learning.
Administer and review an annual assessment of the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning's activities.
Consider and recommend to the Faculty Senate and administration regarding the needs and activities of the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning.
faculty-center-advisory.reportsTo: The Faculty Senate and university administration

campus-safety-security.tldr: Recommends parking, transportation, and campus safety policies for a safer university community.
campus-safety-security.purpose: Evaluates and recommends policies on parking, transportation, and campus safety and security that serve faculty, staff, students, and visitors.
responsibilities: Evaluate and recommend policies and procedures on parking, transportation, and campus safety and security that foster a mutually beneficial environment for faculty, staff, students, and visitors in the university community.
Make recommendations to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
campus-safety-security.reportsTo: The Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate

graduate-policy.tldr: Shapes the policies and procedures that govern graduate education and graduate students.
graduate-policy.purpose: Examines and recommends the policies and procedures that govern graduate education, including admissions, academic progress, and financial support.
responsibilities: Examine existing policies and procedures and recommend new policies and procedures for graduate education, including those affecting admissions, academic progress, and financial support for graduate students.
Review all matters referred by the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
Transmit recommendations to the dean of the College of Graduate Studies (who advises the provost) and to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
graduate-policy.reportsTo: The Graduate Council and the dean of the College of Graduate Studies

personnel.tldr: Recommends academic personnel policy, including promotion, tenure, and chair-evaluation standards.
personnel.purpose: Studies and recommends academic personnel policy and standards, and oversees the evaluation of administrators and department chairs.
responsibilities: Study and recommend academic personnel policy and standards, including promotion and tenure criteria.
Be responsible for procedures to evaluate administrators and department chairs and oversee such evaluations.
Consider other matters related to the professional well-being of the faculty, including academic freedom.
Make recommendations to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
personnel.reportsTo: The Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate

research-council.tldr: Advises on research policy, centers, integrity, and support across the university.
research-council.purpose: Recommends research policy to the Vice President for Research and advises on support for research activities, centers, and integrity.
responsibilities: Recommend policies on research activities, facilities, personnel, and patents to the Vice President for the Office of Research.
Consult with and advise the Vice President for Research on stimulating and supporting research activities, including the allocation of monies invested in UCF research.
Assist in evaluating research programs and advise on new and existing research centers and institutes.
Recommend policies that foster a strong relationship between research centers, institutes, and departments.
Speak for the faculty on governmental, industrial, and other private-sector and foundation support of the university's research programs.
Review the activities of the committees dealing with human subjects, animals, copyrights, and patents in research.
Review and recommend on internal research grants and awards, and promote the responsible conduct of research.
Make recommendations to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
research-council.reportsTo: The Vice President for Research and the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate

undergraduate-policy-curriculum.tldr: Reviews undergraduate academic policy, degree requirements, and new or revised programs.
undergraduate-policy-curriculum.purpose: Studies and recommends university-wide undergraduate academic policy and degree requirements, and reviews proposals for new and revised programs.
responsibilities: Study, review, and recommend on undergraduate academic policies.
Study, review, and recommend on university-wide undergraduate degree requirements, including the General Education Program, foreign language requirements, admission standards, and academic honors.
Review recommendations from the University Common Program Oversight Committee on changes to the General Education Program.
Review and recommend on all proposals for new undergraduate degree programs and minors, and the elimination of existing programs, prior to Board of Trustees approval.
Review and recommend on proposals to revise existing degree programs and certificates.
Recommend on the allocation of the Equipment Fee to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies.
Transmit recommendations to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies and to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
undergraduate-policy-curriculum.reportsTo: The dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies and the Provost

university-travel-awards.tldr: Sets guidelines for and awards faculty travel funding.
university-travel-awards.purpose: Develops guidelines for faculty travel awards and recommends recipients to the Provost.
responsibilities: Develop guidelines for travel awards to faculty.
Announce the availability of and procedures for applying for travel awards.
Solicit applications for travel awards.
Review faculty applications and recommend recipients to the Provost and Vice President for Academic Affairs.
university-travel-awards.reportsTo: The Provost and Vice President for Academic Affairs

admissions-standards.tldr: Hears undergraduate admissions exceptions and student academic appeals.
admissions-standards.purpose: Considers undergraduate admissions that fall below minimum standards and hears student appeals on readmission, academic standing, and late academic actions.
responsibilities: Consider undergraduate admissions that do not meet minimum university standards and are referred by the Office of Undergraduate Admissions.
Consider undergraduate student appeals of readmission decisions or academic standing.
Consider undergraduate appeals of College of Undergraduate Studies decisions on late withdrawals, late drops, late adds, and medical withdrawals.
Consider graduate appeals of College of Graduate Studies decisions on late withdrawals, late drops, late adds, and medical withdrawals.
admissions-standards.reportsTo: The Office of Undergraduate Admissions and the College of Undergraduate Studies

budget-administrative.tldr: Recommends university budget policy and administrative procedures affecting faculty.
budget-administrative.purpose: Evaluates and recommends policies on the university budget — especially the academic budget — and on administrative procedures that affect faculty work.
responsibilities: Evaluate and recommend policies and procedures on the university budget, with special emphasis on the academic budget.
Make recommendations on any administrative procedure that affects faculty in the performance of their work.
Participate in the academic planning process as appropriate.
Make recommendations to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
budget-administrative.reportsTo: The Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate

commencement-convocation-recognitions.tldr: Oversees commencement and convocation practices and reviews honors, awards, and honorary degrees.
commencement-convocation-recognitions.purpose: Reviews commencement and convocation procedures and recommends on speakers, awards, honorary degrees, and other university recognitions.
responsibilities: Examine and review commencement and convocation procedures and recommend on future ceremonies, accounting for budget implications.
Recommend to the President regarding speakers for commencements and convocations.
Recommend on the number and kinds of commencements and convocations.
Review proposals for awards, emeritus status, honorary and posthumous degrees, and other honors, and recommend to the President.
Recommend the standards and criteria for awarding honorary degrees.
Recommend criteria for and serve as the selection committee for the Distinguished Alumnus designation.

course-materials.tldr: Promotes textbook affordability and timely course-material adoption.
course-materials.purpose: Recommends procedures for textbook adoption and promotes a culture that values affordable course materials for students.
responsibilities: Recommend to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies procedures to meet textbook adoption deadlines and reporting requirements.
Promote a culture that values textbook affordability.
Respect faculty expertise in choosing curriculum materials while highlighting cost-effectiveness for students.
Develop scholarship criteria, review applications, and select recipients for free course textbooks and other Bookstore awards each semester.
course-materials.reportsTo: The dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies

faculty-staff-benefits.tldr: Reviews and improves university benefits and wellness programs for faculty and staff.
faculty-staff-benefits.purpose: Reviews and assesses university benefits and wellness programs and recommends improvements that align with the needs of faculty and staff.
responsibilities: Regularly review, compare, and assess university benefits and wellness programs to ensure they meet the evolving needs of faculty and staff.
Collaborate with university stakeholders to develop and implement initiatives that improve faculty and staff benefits and well-being.
Develop and execute strategies to communicate benefit-related updates, initiatives, and resources across the university.
Report findings and recommendations on improving and expanding benefits and wellness programs to the Faculty Senate and/or President.
faculty-staff-benefits.reportsTo: The Faculty Senate and/or the President

student-success-council.tldr: Advances programs, policies, and metrics that help UCF students succeed.
student-success-council.purpose: Promotes programs, policies, and practices that help students succeed academically and personally, and advises university leaders on student success initiatives.
responsibilities: Promote the development and implementation of programs, policies, and practices that help students succeed academically and in their personal well-being.
Advise and assist the University Student Success Steering Committee, the SVP for Student Success, the deans of Undergraduate and Graduate Studies, and related units on student success initiatives.
Review and monitor performance on state performance-based funding, preeminence, and other strategic student success metrics.
Collaborate with the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning and the Center for Distributed Learning on professional development that supports student success.
Serve as an advisory and recommending body to the Faculty Senate on student success strategies, and collaborate with professional advising offices across UCF.
student-success-council.reportsTo: The University Student Success Steering Committee and the Faculty Senate

general-undergraduate-requirements.tldr: Monitors university-wide undergraduate requirements like General Education and diversity.
general-undergraduate-requirements.purpose: Monitors university-wide undergraduate curriculum requirements and recommends on changes to General Education, diversity, and related requirements.
responsibilities: Monitor university-wide undergraduate curriculum requirements such as the General Education Program, diversity, the Gordon Rule, and foreign language proficiency, and review changes to them.
Review and recommend to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies on courses and syllabi submitted to meet the university diversity requirement.
Make recommendations to the Undergraduate Policy and Curriculum Committee on changes to the General Education Program.
Make recommendations to the Undergraduate Course Review Committee on course proposals related to the General Education Program.
general-undergraduate-requirements.reportsTo: The Undergraduate Policy & Curriculum Committee and the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies

graduate-appeals.tldr: Hears graduate student petitions and appeals for exceptions to graduate policy.
graduate-appeals.purpose: Hears petitions and appeals for variances from graduate policies and forwards recommendations to the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
responsibilities: Hear petitions for variances from established policies governing post-baccalaureate, certificate, or graduate degrees at the program, college, or university level.
Hear other cases referred by the dean of the College of Graduate Studies, including appeals of adverse admissions and administrative decisions and appeals under The Golden Rule.
Hear all requests from graduate program directors for exceptions to graduate policies and procedures.
Identify graduate program practices with policy implications and refer them to the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
Review all matters referred by the Graduate Council; conclusions are recommendations to the dean, who makes the final decision.
graduate-appeals.reportsTo: The dean of the College of Graduate Studies

graduate-curriculum.tldr: Reviews graduate course and program curriculum changes.
graduate-curriculum.purpose: Reviews curricular changes for graduate education — courses, tracks, options, and certificate programs — and recommends to the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
responsibilities: Review curricular issues related to graduate education and recommend to the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
Review changes to existing graduate programs, such as hours and thesis/non-thesis options.
Review new tracks or options for existing graduate programs and the deletion of tracks or options.
Review new certificate programs and the deletion of existing certificate programs.
Review all requests for additions, revisions, and deletions of graduate and special-topic courses.
Review all matters referred by the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
graduate-curriculum.reportsTo: The dean of the College of Graduate Studies

graduate-program-review-awards.tldr: Reviews graduate programs and selects graduate teaching, thesis, and dissertation awards.
graduate-program-review-awards.purpose: Reviews graduate programs as part of the program review cycle and selects recipients for graduate student and teaching awards.
responsibilities: Review graduate programs in the program review cycle, identify factors that promote quality, and update faculty status for participating faculty; report findings to the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
Review proposals for new graduate programs and the deletion of existing programs submitted by the dean of the College of Graduate Studies.
Review nominees for graduate student awards in teaching, thesis, and dissertation, and for the Excellence in Graduate Teaching Award, and recommend to the dean.
graduate-program-review-awards.reportsTo: The dean of the College of Graduate Studies

information-technology.tldr: Advises on faculty information-technology needs and university IT policy.
information-technology.purpose: Assesses faculty information-technology needs and advises the Vice President for Information Technologies on resources that enhance teaching, learning, research, and service.
responsibilities: Assist in determining and evaluating faculty information technology needs and review general policies in this area.
Review changes to educational media and communications recommended by the Vice President for Information Technologies and Resources.
Advise the Vice President for Information Technologies and Resources on developing IT resources that enhance teaching, learning, research, and service.
Make recommendations to the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate.
information-technology.reportsTo: The Vice President for Information Technologies and the Steering Committee of the Faculty Senate

library-advisory.tldr: Connects faculty with the Libraries and advises Library administration.
library-advisory.purpose: Facilitates communication between the UCF Libraries and faculty and advises Library administration.
responsibilities: Facilitate communication between the Libraries and their academic clientele.
Act as advocates of the Libraries in university-wide decision-making groups.
Act as an advisory council to the Libraries administration.

strategic-planning-council.tldr: Helps develop and update the university's strategic and academic plan.
strategic-planning-council.purpose: Advises and assists the Provost in developing and updating the university's strategic plan and academic plan.
responsibilities: Advise and assist the Provost and Vice President for Academic Affairs in developing and updating the university's strategic plan.
Recommend policies that support strategic planning, including academic planning, institutional effectiveness, accountability, budget planning, and student services.
Recommend the university's academic plan and any budget or academic actions needed to support it to the Provost.
strategic-planning-council.reportsTo: The Provost and Vice President for Academic Affairs

undergraduate-course-review.tldr: Reviews all undergraduate course additions, revisions, and deletions.
undergraduate-course-review.purpose: Reviews undergraduate course actions and special-topics requests and recommends them to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies.
responsibilities: Review all undergraduate course additions, revisions, deletions, and special-topics requests, and transmit recommendations to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies.
Review recommendations from the Undergraduate Common Program Oversight Committee on course proposals related to the General Education Program.
Recommend on the allocation of the Material and Supplies Fee to the dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies.
undergraduate-course-review.reportsTo: The dean of the College of Undergraduate Studies

athletics-advisory.tldr: Safeguards academic integrity and student-athlete welfare in UCF Athletics.
athletics-advisory.purpose: Provides academic oversight of University Athletics and safeguards student-athlete welfare, academic progress, and NCAA compliance.
responsibilities: Nurture and foster academic integrity through oversight of academic matters related to University Athletics.
Supervise the university's relationship with its athletic conference and the NCAA and ensure compliance with their rules and regulations.
Review the academic advisement, retention, and admissions of athletes.
Recommend to the Faculty Senate policies that ensure the academic integrity of athletic programs.
Serve as an advisory body on policies that relate to student-athlete welfare and academic progress.
athletics-advisory.reportsTo: The Faculty Senate and University Athletics

university-honors.tldr: Recommends policy and curriculum for the Burnett Honors College.
university-honors.purpose: Recommends fundamental policy, curriculum, and student-affairs decisions for the Burnett Honors College.
responsibilities: Consider and recommend fundamental policies and procedures for the Burnett Honors College, including its direction, programs, requirements, faculty participation, and curriculum.
Recommend to the dean of the Burnett Honors College on faculty and student awards, scholarships, and other benefits.
Recommend to the dean on disciplinary matters, including the removal of a student from the program.
university-honors.reportsTo: The dean of the Burnett Honors College

university-master-planning.tldr: Reviews campus land use, facilities planning, and aesthetics.
university-master-planning.purpose: Reviews long- and short-range campus land use, facilities planning, and aesthetics, and advises the President on campus development.
responsibilities: Review short- and long-range issues related to land use, facilities planning, and future campus development, including the protection of natural resources.
Recommend to the President on aesthetics and suitability for minor projects and modifications to the campus landscape, utilities, and building exteriors.
Review signage, site furniture, public art, and some temporary installations.
Serve as a clearinghouse for communication to the campus community.

university-promotion-tenure.tldr: Conducts university-level review of faculty promotion and tenure cases.
university-promotion-tenure.purpose: Conducts the university-level review of faculty promotion and tenure cases and helps maintain consistent university-wide P&T standards.
responsibilities: Review promotion and tenure recommendations forwarded from the colleges for consistency with university standards.
Make recommendations on candidates to the Provost and Vice President for Academic Affairs.
Advise on university-wide promotion and tenure criteria and procedures, in coordination with the Personnel Committee's policy work.
university-promotion-tenure.reportsTo: The Provost and Vice President for Academic Affairs
